final_prompt.prompt:
Create an architectural photography image of a karesansui (dry landscape) garden at a renowned Zen Buddhist temple in Kyoto, Japan.

Style/medium: wide-angle architectural photography, low-angle perspective, sharp foreground-to-midground depth, in the spirit of Kengo Kuma's spatial documentation and Hiroshi Sugimoto's meditative precision
Composition: low-angle wide shot emphasizing geometric raked patterns and spatial recession, wide-angle lens (16–24mm equivalent), strong leading lines from raked gravel extending toward background wall
Subject: expansive karesansui garden — white-grey gravel meticulously raked into concentric circles and wave-like ripple patterns symbolizing ocean and waves; several moss-covered rocks of varied forms arranged asymmetrically, representing islands and mountain peaks
Scene/backdrop: enclosed Zen temple garden in Kyoto; low wooden veranda corridors on the perimeter with evenly spaced pillars; aged earthen wall (土壁) in the background topped with a strip of green moss; autumn afternoon setting
Lighting/mood: low-angle autumn afternoon sunlight casting long elongated shadows from rocks and veranda pillars across the raked gravel; warm directional sidelight; deeply tranquil, contemplative, and serene Zen aesthetic
Color palette: warm off-white and pale sand tones dominate the gravel; deep moss green anchors the rocks and wall cap; aged timber brown for the veranda; muted, restrained overall tonality with no saturated colors
Materials/textures: fine raked gravel with clearly visible rake lines and concentric grooves; rough weathered rock surfaces with dense moss growth; worn unpainted wood grain on pillars; crumbling earthen wall texture
Avoid: tourists or human figures, modern elements, harsh midday flat light, oversaturated colors, lens flare, digital HDR halos, watermark
